raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS - A white napkin is placed on a patterned tablecloth. The napkin is a flat, rectangular object with a soft texture. STRATEGY - The task is to pull the napkin from the table. Since the napkin is already on the table, the action involves grasping it and lifting it away from the surface. Failure modes include not grasping the napkin properly or lifting it too high, causing it to fall. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the napkin at its edge for a stable grasp; Step 2 grasp the edge to lift it; Step 3 transport it to a new location.**
Goal: any_in(napkin, hand); stable(napkin)
Scene: napkin, table
Step 1: reach(napkin)
  affordance_region: the edge of the white napkin
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, napkin, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, napkin) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(napkin)
  affordance_region: the edge of the white napkin
  contact: holding(napkin) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(napkin)
Step 3: transport(napkin -> hand)
  affordance_region: an empty area in the hand
  spatial: above(napkin, hand, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(napkin)
  done_when: above(napkin, hand, 0.05)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the edge of the white napkin
the edge of the white napkin
an empty area in the hand
